DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF ARASH GHANADAN'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY OF PEACE OFFICER PERSONNEL RECORDS IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTED DOCUMENTS REGARDING ALLEGED FALSE ARRESTS AND/OR IMPRISONMENT BY OFFICER BERTRAND IN THE PRIOR FIVE YEARS. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT DOES NOT PLEAD EXCESSIVE USE OF FORCE OR ILLEGAL SEARCH AND SEIZURE, SO THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THOSE AND OTHER SUBJECT MATTERS. DEFENDANTS ARE TO PROVIDE, WITHIN 14 DAYS, COPIES OF ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW BY THIS COURT (IN DEPARTMENT 503). JUDGE RICHARD ULMER. NOT REPORTED.

POS OF WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE PROFFERED BY DEFT'S OPPOS. TO DEFT'S MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF GHANADAN, ARASH AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE CHIEF GEORGE GASCON, AND SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICER LARRY BERTRAND'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS BURDEN AS TO BERTRAND BUT NOT CHIEF GASCON OR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, PLAINTIFF RAISES TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT: (1) WHEN PLAINTIFF WAS ARRESTED; (2) WHETHER PROBABLE CAUSE EXISTED FOR THE ARREST; AND (3) WHETHER OFFICER BERTRAND MAY HAVE KNOWN THAT PLAINTIFF WAS (OR WAS NOT) IN CHARGE OF THE PARTY. (BERTRAND DEPOSITION, EVANS DECLARATION, GHEZLAOUI DECLARATION). TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL RIGHT TO FILE A COMPLAINT WITH THE O C C WAS INTERFERED WITH BY THE "NOW YOU'LL SEE WHAT HAPPENS..." COMMENT. (DECLARATION OF HOMIDI). PLAINTIFF MAY PLEAD INTENTIONAL TORTS AND NEGLIGENCE IN THE ALTERNATIVE. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER THE C C S F AND POLICE CHIEF GASCON RETAINED OFFICER BERTRAND KNOWING THAT HE POSED A DANGER. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS ON DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE: EXHIBITS E, F & G OVERRULED; DECLARATION OF GHANADAN OVERRULED. (COURT DOES NOT RULE ON IMMUNITY ISSUE BASED ON COURT'S RULING. COURT DENIED DEFENDANTS REQUEST RE DISCOVERY ISSUE.) PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S ORDER RE: OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANTS' EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF THEIR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION

Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, Officer Larry Bertrand And Chief Of Police George Gascon'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE CHIEF GEORGE GASCON, AND SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICER LARRY BERTRAND'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Rory C. Quintana, Esq. & Samantha R. Nilsen, Esq. of Kumin Sommers LLP 434-4500 for pltf & opposing party; Robert A. Bonta, Deputy City Atty 554-4268 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied. Defendant shifts burden as to Bertrand but not Chief Gascon or the City and County of San Francisco, plaintiff raises triable issues of material fact: (1) when plaintiff was arrested; (2) whether probable cause existed for the arrest; and (3) whether Officer Bertrand may have known that plaintiff was (or was not) in charge of the party. (Bertrand deposition, Evans declaration, Ghezlaoui declaration). Triable issue of fact raised whether plaintiff's legal right to file a complaint with the O C C was interfered with by the "Now you'll see what happens..." comment. (declaration of Homidi). Plaintiff may plead intentional torts and negligence in the alternative. Triable issue of fact raised whether the C C S F and Police Chief Gascon retained Officer Bertrand knowing that he posed a danger. The Court ruled as follows on defendants objections to plaintiff's evidence: Exhibits E, F & G overruled; declaration of Ghanadan overruled. (Court does not rule on immunity issue based on Court's ruling. Court denied defendants request re discovery issue.) Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. Plaintiff's order re: objections to evidence signed. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ARASH GHANADAN DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Samantha R. Nilsen, Esq. of Kuman Sommers LLP (415) 434-4500 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Robert A. Bonta, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4268 appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first affirmative defense (failure to state a cause of action) which is an objection, not a defense, and is not waived. CCP section 430.80(a) Demurrer sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to remaining defenses to specify facts that support the defenses. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discovery Of Peace Officer Personnel Records; (Evidence Code 1043 And 1045);
Off Calendar Feb-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Samantha R. Nilsen, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: ARASH GHANADAN
Robert A. Bonta, DCA, for Defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF ARASH GHANADAN's motion for Discovery of Peace Officer Personnel records is GRANTED as to requested documents regarding alleged false arrests and/or imprisonment by Officer Bertrand in the prior five years. Plaintiff's complaint does not plead excessive use of force or illegal search and seizure, so the motion is DENIED as to those and other subject matters. Defendants are to provide, within 14 days, copies of all responsive documents for in camera review by this Court (in Department 503).
Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.
